valid.name: no-rule
valid.code:
CREATE TRIGGER t_trg BEFORE UPDATE ON t FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION t_trg_fn();
CREATE VIEW v AS SELECT * FROM t;

invalid.name: create-rule
invalid.code: CREATE RULE noop AS ON UPDATE TO t DO INSTEAD NOTHING;

errors.message: Avoid `CREATE RULE`. PostgreSQL's rule system has surprising semantics around row counts, RETURNING, and updatable views; use a trigger or an updatable view instead.
